easy.title: Screen Reader Optimization Audit
easy.description: Take a provided HTML page and optimize it for screen reader support. Add proper heading hierarchy, descriptive link text, form labels, alt text for images, ARIA landmarks, and a skip link. Document each change and explain which screen reader feature it improves.

Screen reader improvements: 1. Skip link for keyboard users 2. Semantic landmarks (header, nav, main, article, section) 3. Proper heading hierarchy (h1, h2, h3) 4. Descriptive link text 5. Image alt text 6. Form labels 7. Proper submit button (not a span) 8. lang attribute 9. Descriptive page title

medium.title: Build a Screen Reader-Friendly Dashboard
medium.description: Build a data dashboard page that is fully optimized for screen readers. Include: a data table with associated headers (scope, caption), keyboard-navigable chart controls with accessible descriptions, a form for filtering data with labels and validation announcements, live region updates for data refresh, proper heading hierarchy, ARIA landmarks, skip links, and focus management. All interactive elements must be fully operable with keyboard alone.
medium.starterCode: Dashboard
medium.solution:
Full dashboard with:

1. Accessible data table: <caption>, <thead>/<tbody>, scope="col" and scope="row", summary attribute
2. Filter controls: <label> for each input, <fieldset>/<legend> for groups, aria-describedby for help text
3. Live region: aria-live="polite" for data refresh announcements, role="status" for status
4. Keyboard navigation: Tab order follows logical flow, focus indicators visible
5. Headings: h1 dashboard title, h2 for each widget, h3 for sub-sections
6. Landmarks: role="main", role="navigation", role="complementary"
7. Skip link: jumps to main table
8. Chart fallback: data table available for chart visualization
9. Form validation: aria-invalid, aria-describedby for errors, role="alert" for announcements
10. Focus management: after filter apply, focus moves to updated results

hard.title: Screen Reader-Compatible SPA Framework
hard.description: Build a minimal single-page application framework that prioritizes screen reader support. Requirements: announce route changes via aria-live, manage focus on all route transitions, maintain proper heading hierarchy, persist focus position across renders, handle loading/failed states with proper announcements, implement proper keyboard navigation for lists and navigation, support the CSS .sr-only pattern, and include a testing script for NVDA/VoiceOver validation. Create a demo app with at least 3 routes demonstrating all features.
